svg.text: AgentKeys key map — how keys are generated, bound, and why privacy & security hold
Worked example: agentkeys init --email test@litentry.org, then pair agent "hermes". All hashes real; the wallet is illustrative (only the TEE can produce it). Companion to arch.md §4.1.
used once at bootstrap, then discarded;
never stored on chain, S3, or in J1
2 · SIGNER TEE — sealed; K3 never leaves
derived on demand, RAM only — never persisted, never logged:
broker, daemon, workers, even host root: none can read K3 or K4
— they only see addresses and signatures
= the wallet's own identity omni — email not in the preimage
public + recomputable by design — zero secrets;
knowing an omni grants NOTHING (see bindings ↓)
registry + scope rows keyed by omnis
signs every request; no biometric needed
Touch ID gesture; cannot be exfiltrated,
5 · ON-CHAIN — single source of truth, default-deny
a paired device with no scope grant can authenticate
unpair = revoke the BINDING (omnis are facts, not grants)
The email exists only inside the transient identity_omni. Chain rows, S3 paths, JWTs and
audit rows carry wallet-derived omnis — one-way SHA-256, email not in any stored preimage.
Root secret sealed; nothing derived is stored.
K3 never leaves the TEE; every wallet/KEK is re-derived per use and never persisted or logged.
Compromising broker, daemon, workers or host shell yields addresses, not keys.
Omnis carry zero secrets — anyone can compute O_hermes, nobody can bind to it: a binding
requires the master's J1-gated pairing claim plus the master's K11 Touch ID UserOp on chain.
Each actor gets its own K4 wallet, S3 prefix (enforced by IAM PrincipalTag), scope row and audit
identity. A compromised hermes VM touches bots/a152e6b3…/ only — master and siblings unreachable.
K10 signs every request frictionlessly; the hardware-sealed K11 (Touch ID) is demanded only
for mutations — device add/revoke, scope grant/revoke. Theft of K10 alone cannot re-permission.
Chain is the source of truth, default-deny, checked twice.
No scope row → no JWT, no cap. The broker checks the chain at mint AND every worker
independently re-verifies device + scope + epoch per call — a lying broker changes nothing.
OIDC 300 s, STS 1 h, session 5 h — leaks age out fast. K3 epoch bump re-keys every wallet while
the frozen anchors keep S3 / IAM / scope / AAD untouched: rotation is a zero-migration event.
Defense in depth, end to end.
Identity (transient) → TEE-held secrets → public anchors → chain-gated bindings → short-lived
bearers → per-actor IAM. Each layer assumes the previous one failed.
Formulas pinned by tests: crates/agentkeys-core/src/actor_omni.rs · crates/agentkeys-broker-server/src/identity/omni_account.rs · key inventory: docs/arch.md §4–§6
Generated 2026-06-11 · source of truth is arch.md §4.1 (mermaid) — keep this illustration in sync when the derivations change
